Prepared by Shore Excursions International · March 2026

The New Boat Tour
Opportunity

A business opportunity proposal prepared by Shore Excursions International for Rhino Tours St. Maarten — outlining a new captain-led boat excursion product, its market fit, competitive pricing, inclusions benchmark, and the path to launching at Seatrade.

Presented to Andres Stephany & Tim McCalep
Partnership Shore Excursions International & Rhino Tours
Target Event Seatrade Cruise Global 2026
Status Pre-Pitch Review

01

The Three Tour Concepts

Concept A — Recommended Lead
Grand Case
Beach Escape
📍 Depart Philipsburg → coastal route north → Grand Case Beach, beach bars & French restaurants
🛡️ Protected route via marina available in bad weather; Kimsha fallback if rough seas
🍽️ Authentic French Caribbean atmosphere — dining options on-site for guests at own expense
✈️ Optional scenic pass of Maho Beach (low-flying planes) on outbound leg — more fuel required
⏱️ Timetable TBD — Tim to run route test on Rhino to confirm realistic durations
NEW DESTINATION NOT IN CARNIVAL/NCL CATALOG
Concept B
Pinel Island
Day Tour
📍 Depart Philipsburg → Pinel Island — secluded, photogenic, French side
🤿 Crystal-clear shallow water, snorkeling, beach relaxation — strong Instagram appeal
⚠️ More open sea exposure than Grand Case route — weather sensitivity is higher
🍽️ Limited on-site food/drink infrastructure compared to Grand Case
⏱️ Timetable TBD — Tim to scout and confirm logistics
NEW DESTINATION
Concept C
Captain-Led
Rhino Tour
📍 Same Rhino Tour route — lagoon, coastline, beach stop — but captain-driven, up to 10 pax per boat
🙌 A completely different guest profile — couples, older guests, non-drivers, and anyone who wants the Rhino experience without taking the wheel
💡 Self-drive and captain-led appeal to different mindsets; there is no meaningful overlap in who books each product
🌴 St. Maarten's on-water options are genuinely limited — more variety on the water benefits the destination and strengthens the Rhino Tours portfolio overall
⏱️ Timetable and route to be confirmed alongside Concepts A & B
COMPLEMENTARY PRODUCT
↔
CONCEPT D — CAPACITY MULTIPLIER HIGH PRIORITY CONSIDERATION
Back-to-Back Hybrid: Boat ↔ Bus

Rather than running a single group on the boat tour, operate two groups simultaneously in a synchronized back-to-back rotation — effectively doubling the revenue per time slot with the same capital assets.

GROUP A
🚤 Boat → 🚌 Bus
Departs pier by boat → scenic coastal ride to Grand Case → beach time, bar, restaurants → returns to Philipsburg by air-conditioned bus (via Marigot, French side sights, island highlights)
⇄
GROUP B
🚌 Bus → 🚤 Boat
Departs pier by air-conditioned bus → island highlights, Marigot, French side → arrives Grand Case → beach time, bar, restaurants → returns to Philipsburg by boat
CAPACITY IMPACT
Same single boat serves 20 guests per time slot instead of 10 — effectively doubles revenue per trip without a second vessel
GUEST EXPERIENCE
Every guest gets both modes — boat AND bus — making this a richer, more complete island experience than either alone
ALREADY SOLVED ✓
Bus coordination can be handled by Dutch Tours, Rhino Tours' existing transfer partner — a proven relationship that makes this hybrid format immediately viable from day one
OPERATION TIMELINE — 5 GROUPS · 4h00 EACH · 1 BOAT
Every guest: 2 bus rides + 1 boat ride · departs and returns to cruise ship port

Pitch angle for cruise lines: This hybrid format is unique in the St. Maarten market — no competitor offers a combined boat+bus Grand Case experience. It's also a stronger sell at the $97–$109 retail tier because guests perceive higher value getting two modes of transport and two perspectives of the island in a single excursion.
02

The Market Opportunity

✅ Opportunities
True white space. Neither Carnival nor NCL offers any captain-led boat excursion to Grand Case or Pinel Island. The French north coast by sea is completely absent from all cruise line water excursion catalogs.
Format fills a gap. Existing water tours are either self-drive (Rhino), large catamarans (40–80 pax), or premium schooners ($129). A 10-pax captain-led boat sits in the middle — intimate but not exclusive-charter pricing.
Existing relationships with all major cruise lines. SEI already has contracted, operational relationships with Carnival, Royal Caribbean, Holland America, NCL, and Virgin Voyages. This is not a cold pitch — it's a new product offering to partners who already know and trust the operation.
Seatrade timing. Tim is already ordering the boat regardless. The strategy of "sell it at Seatrade, then buy additional boats" is low-risk — no stranded capital unless a deal is struck.
Bulk engine leverage. Tim's full fleet order (8 vessels + 10+ Rhino engines) creates real negotiating power to reduce per-engine cost on the twin 115 HP units.
⚠️ Challenges
Inclusions gap vs. market. Every comparable water tour at the $93–$129 Carnival/NCL price tier includes an open bar. The current Rhino — and by extension this new product — includes only water. This is a known gap that needs addressing.
Weather sensitivity. The proposed boat is not well-suited for rough seas. Grand Case route has a protected marina option, but Pinel has more open-sea exposure. This must be addressed in every proposal with a clear bad-weather fallback.
Timetables are not ready. Andres correctly flagged that realistic timing needs a test run on a Rhino. Without actual departure/return times, the proposals cannot be finalized. This is Tim's near-term action item.
Capital exposure if unsold. At ~$40K per boat, buying multiple vessels before contracts are signed is a significant risk. The strategy must remain: pitch at Seatrade first, commit capital after.

🆕 SEI New Boat Tour (Proposed)
Grand Case or Pinel — Captain-led, 10 pax
SEI
~$97–109
25-ft boat, captain-led, 10 pax
TBD No Bar Currently
⚠️ Inclusions gap: At the $97–$109 retail price point where Carnival would list this tour, every single competing water excursion includes at minimum an open bar. The Rhino self-drive is the only product at this tier without one — and even it includes a beach chair and snorkel mask. For the new boat tour to be competitively listed and to meet guest expectations at this price, some form of beverage inclusion is strongly recommended — at minimum water, sodas, and a rum punch.
💡 Pricing logic: SEI currently sells the Rhino to Carnival at $65/person. Carnival retails it at $97.99 — a ~51% markup. Keeping the same operator rate for the new boat tour means cruise lines would retail it at approximately $97–$109, consistent with their current water tour tier and well within what guests expect to pay. Going above $65 at the operator level would push the retail price into $150+ territory, where competition from fully-inclusive products like the Spirit of St. Maarten schooner ($129 with lunch and open bar) becomes a real headwind.
04

Capital Requirements

🛥️ Per-Boat Cost Estimate
Hull — base price (no options) ~$11,000
Hull — with premium options ~$19,000
Twin 115 HP outboard engines (×2) ~$18,000–$23,000
Shipping (if in Tim's container) ~$500
Shipping (standalone) ~$7,000
Total Estimated Range $35,000–$45,000
💡 Ship in Tim's container — saving $6,500 vs. standalone shipping. Only viable if timed with Tim's current order.
📊 Revenue Potential Per Boat
Operator rate to cruise line $65 / person
Capacity per trip 10 passengers
Revenue per full trip $650
Est. trips per ship call (2 trips) $1,300
Est. fuel + captain cost per trip TBD by Tim
Full P&L Rhino Tours to size
Note: Operational costs — captain salary, fuel, drinks inclusion, and maintenance — need to be quantified by Rhino Tours to complete the business case. SEI is ready to support with cruise line pricing benchmarks.
05

What's Needed Before Seatrade

📋 Recommended Next Steps for Rhino Tours
Run a test route on a Rhino to Grand Case and Pinel to establish realistic departure, transit, and return times — the critical input needed to finalize all three proposals
Marina access confirmed. Rhino Tours already uses the marina at Grand Case through existing operations — no new arrangements required
Estimate fuel cost per trip and captain day rate to build an operating cost baseline
Provide photos and video footage of Grand Case (Rainbow Beach Bar already in hand) and Pinel for pitch materials
🤝 SEI Commitments
Build the three proposal layouts with timetables, descriptions, photos, and pricing once Tim provides operational data
Decide on inclusions package — recommend minimum: water + sodas + rum punch to meet market expectations at the $97–$109 retail tier
Determine priority line for lead pitch — Holland America (best demographic fit for Grand Case) or Carnival (highest volume)
Seatrade meetings already secured. SEI has confirmed meetings at Seatrade with all cruise lines currently operating Rhino Tours — plus key prospects — making this the ideal moment to introduce the new product
🌊 Weather & Risk Plan
Primary route: Via marina (protected) both ways — faster, no bridge timing issues, minimizes open-sea exposure
Scenic option: Pass Maho on outbound (more fuel, more ocean) — offered only when conditions allow
Bad weather fallback: Redirect to Kimsha Beach (fully protected, close to port) — same beach break format, no cancellation needed
Extreme weather: Full cancellation with refund — standard cruise line policy already in place for existing tours
Bottom Line

This is a legitimate opportunity. The product fills a genuine white space in the St. Maarten cruise excursion market — no competing captain-led boat tour goes to Grand Case or Pinel. The pricing logic works at the existing $65 operator rate. The strategy of pitching at Seatrade before committing capital is exactly right. The two things that need to be resolved before this can be sold: realistic timetables from a test run, and a decision on beverage inclusions to match market expectations at the ~$100 retail tier.

ACTION 01
Add a basic bar package — water, sodas, and rum punch at minimum. Every competing boat tour at this price includes it. This is the single highest-impact change to make the product competitive.
ACTION 02
Lead with Grand Case. More to do, better food scene, more weather-protected routing, stronger photos already in hand. Pinel is a strong second concept but harder to operationalize quickly.
ACTION 03
Sell first, buy second. Tim is already buying one boat — that's the proof of concept. Only commission additional hulls after a cruise line contract is signed at Seatrade.
PREPARED BY SEI · FOR RHINO TOURS ST. MAARTEN · MARCH 2026
